response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer with strong testing culture and some systems-level background, but his professional C++ experience amounts to a single seven-month stint in 2014-2015, and his last decade has centered on TypeScript/Node.js web development and DLang. The role demands current, hands-on C++/STL expertise, industrial-protocol knowledge (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), and IEC 62443 secure-development practice — none of which appear on this resume. His low-latency, event-driven DLang work at Sociomantic is conceptually adjacent, but the core stack and industrial-cybersecurity requirements are unmet.
strengths: Nearly two decades of broad engineering experience, including low-latency, asynchronous, high-throughput systems work (DLang at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Event-driven and distributed-systems exposure via microservices handling millions of requests per second and an open-source geo-data platform
Strong mentoring, testing, and software-craftsmanship record (TDD, workshops, courses, open-source test tooling)
concerns: Professional C++ experience is minimal and dated (~7 months at National Instruments ending in 2015); C++ is absent from his own skills list
No industrial automation, IEC 62443/SDL, or protocol experience (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, DNP 3.0) anywhere in the background
Recent roles are web/full-stack TypeScript product work, a significant retooling distance from high-performance native C++ development

key_factors.factor: Lack of current hands-on C++/STL expertise
No industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443) or IoT protocol experience
